channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: World Cup final: Messi may equal Ronaldo’s unique football record
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Lionel Messi could match Ronaldo’s unique football record of winning at least two World Cups and two Ballon d’Or awards if Argentina win the 2026 final.
Read More: https://punchng.com/world-cup-final-messi-may-equal-ronaldos-unique-football-record/

item.title: Russia says Ukrainian drone attack kills seven, at logistics centre
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
A Ukrainian drone attack on a logistics centre in western Russia killed seven people and wounded 24. This Ukraine drone attack targeted a Wildberries facil
Read More: https://punchng.com/russia-says-ukrainian-drone-attack-kills-seven-at-logistics-centre/

item.title: PHOTO: Burna Boy, Shakira meet ahead of World Cup final halftime show
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerian Afrobeats star Burna Boy and pop icon Shakira meet during rehearsals for the 2026 FIFA World Cup final halftime show.
Read More: https://punchng.com/photo-burna-boy-shakira-meet-ahead-of-world-cup-final-halftime-show/

item.title: NUPRC tasks fresh licence holders on rapid field development
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NUPRC charges new petroleum licence holders from the 2022/2023 Mini Bid Round and 2024 Licensing Round to rapidly develop assets to meet oil targets.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nuprc-tasks-fresh-licence-holders-on-rapid-field-development/

item.title: Tinubu signs crypto, virtual assets regulation order
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
President Tinubu signs an Executive Order to regulate crypto and virtual assets in Nigeria, strengthening oversight and protecting investors from financial
Read More: https://punchng.com/tinubu-signs-crypto-virtual-assets-regulation-order/

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Trump reveals why England lost to Argentina in semi-final
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: United States president, Donald Trump, has taken a swipe at what he believes were defensive tactics from England manager, Thomas Tuchel, as they lost 2-1 to England in the semi-final of the 2026 World Cup. The Three Lions took the lead through Anthony Gordon’s goal in the second half. But the

item.title: 2027: How Running Mates Could Swing Votes For Tinubu, Atiku, Obi
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The unveiling of vice-presidential candidates by the major political parties has shifted attention to the possible impact of running mates on the outcome of the 2027 presidential election. With the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) closing nominations, President Bola Tinubu of the rul

item.title: Tinubu’s reforms driving national infrstructural development, says Uzodimma
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “Our people have already decided that they want Tinubu back as president because without him and his policies, this environment we are enjoying today wouldn’t be possible,” he added.

item.title: AC Milan Lead Race To Sign Gabriel Jesus
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Serie A giants AC Milan have emerged as the frontrunners to sign Arsenal striker Gabriel Jesus during the current transfer window. Reports circulating via AFC Xtra indicate that the Rossoneri are now the strongest candidates to secure the Brazilian forward’s signature, with the Italian club stepping

item.title: From lender to borrower: Arise TV’s Rufai Oseni weeps over Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria that once had the resources to bankroll the World Bank and the IMF now finds itself among the world’s most persistent borrowers The post From lender to borrower: Arise TV’s Rufai Oseni weeps over Nigeria appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Our lives are in danger’ – Owerri residents lament as flood takes over homes
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Residents of Umuguma Federal Housing Estate in Owerri West LGA Imo State have cried out to the State Government and other relevant authorities as flood takes over their homes. The residents are appealing for immediate remedial measures from the Government to contain the devastating effects of the fl

item.title: Tems becomes first female African artiste to earn Diamond single in US
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Nigerian singer, Temilade Openiyi, popularly known as Tems, has become the first female African artist to earn a Diamond single in the United States. This is her collaborative hit with Future and Drake, ‘Wait For U,’ was recently certified Diamond by the Recording Industry Association of

item.title: Case for replicating the Oyo kidnap-rescue template nationwide
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Because human beings are prone to perceive nonexistent patterns, connections and intentional design even in random or unrelated events, a cognitive tendency called apophenia, and because Nigerians have an enduring and justified mistrust of government, I have seen many people question whether the abd

item.title: Rogers Pushes For Arsenal Switch As Villa Talks Gather Pace
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Aston Villa forward Morgan Rogers has reportedly informed the club’s hierarchy of his desire to complete a summer move to Arsenal, a development that has accelerated negotiations between the two Premier League clubs. According to reports from AFC Xtra, the England international has made his intentio

item.title: World Cup 2026: England Must Learn To Close The Gap On Spain, Argentina, France
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel has admitted that a gap remains between the Three Lions and serial tournament winners like Argentina, France, and Spain. Speaking ahead of the FIFA World Cup 2026 third-place playoff, Tuchel noted that traditional powerhouses like Argentina and France have established t

item.title: China Urges Better Cooperation With Kyrgyzstan on Green Minerals
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China urged stronger cooperation with Kyrgyzstan across the green minerals industrial chain, while calling for the protection of the rights and interests of Chinese companies in the country, according to a statement from the Ministry of Commerce on Saturday.

item.title: How Aboyeji built $2b companies from Lagos
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Iyinoluwa Aboyeji, who co-founded Andela and Flutterwave, two technology companies that have each achieved valuations of over $1 billion. The post How Aboyeji built $2b companies from Lagos appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Good morning! Here is today’s summary from Nigerian Newspapers: 1.Troops of the Joint Task Force North West, Operation Fansan Yamma, have rescued nine victims who escaped from their abductors, recovered 56 rustled livestock and arrested a suspected terrorist during separate intelligence-led operatio

item.title: Middle East live: Tehran threatens 'full-scale' offensive as US launches seventh night of attacks
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: The US launched a seventh wave of airstrikes on Iran overnight on Friday despite a warning from Tehran that it would resume "full-scale offensive operations" if the attacks continued. Follow our liveblog for the latest updates.

item.title: China’s Siemens Competitor Eyes Buying Its Way Into Europe
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Shenzhen Inovance Technology Co. Ltd. is considering acquisitions in Europe, signaling the Chinese company is readying a push to compete in Europe with industrial incumbents like Siemens AG and top auto supplier Robert Bosch GmbH.

item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Arsenal defender Nacho Monreal has named Morgan Rogers as one of the wingers Arsenal should sign this summer, stating that the Aston Villa man possesses all the necessary qualities to make a massive impact for the Gunners. Monreal praised the 23-year-old’s breakout performances, advocating th

item.title: India Moves Against Youth Protesters Before Parliament Session
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Indian authorities moved against protesters in New Delhi ahead of demonstrations against unemployment and alleged examination scams, as the movement adds pressure on Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s government.

item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

item.title: China Solar Cell Exports Extend Decline in June
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s solar cell exports fell for a second month in June, an indication that overseas demand is weakening after a surge in shipments earlier in the year.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China said it hopes Indonesia can provide a stable and transparent policy environment for its mineral sector, as the two countries deepen economic ties.

item.description: Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt dupedSitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully

item.description: The Philippines protests China Daily videos depicting Filipinos as monkeys amid South China Sea tensions.

item.title: At least 14 dead after minibus rolls off mountain road in Peru
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: At least 14 people have been killed and five injured after a minibus in the Peruvian Andes lost control on a curve.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Protesters clashed with police in Venice outside US Ambassador Tilman Fertitta’s superyacht.

item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Police said investigation revealed that the deceased was allegedly accused of stealing chickens and eggs on April 19 from a poultry farm located along Mbak 4 Community in Uyo Local Government Area.

item.title: Anambra teacher declared wanted for ‘flogging’ student to death
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The Command expressed deep concern over the tragic incident and assured the family of the deceased and members of the public that it would conduct a thorough, diligent, and transparent investigation on the matter.

item.title: PDP leadership battle: Court fines Wabara, others N140m, awards N10m against lawyer
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The judge, who affirmed the leadership of Abdulrahman-led PDP, the faction loyal to the FCT minister, said the reliefs sought by the plaintiffs were not grantable.

item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Sadiq Abdullateef Sir: Nigeria’s journey toward Universal Health Coverage has often been slowed by familiar barriers: low insurance penetration, high out-of-pocket spending, weak trust in public systems, and the difficulty of reaching poor and vulnerable households before illness pushes The post

item.dc:creator: Guardian NG Opinion
item.description: By Emmanuel Nzomiwu Although Dr. Michael Iheonukara Okpara died almost 42 years ago, the mention of his name in Nigeria today, evokes memories of the agricultural and agro-industrial revolution in Eastern Nigeria in the 1950s and 60s, during his days The post SEDC: Reinventing Michael Okpara’s agric

item.description: The killing of the Benue State Chairman of the Miyetti Allah Cattle Breeders Association of Nigeria (MACBAN), Ardo Mohammed, and his associate, Yakubu Idaho, is yet another tragic reminder of the fragile security situation in parts of the country. item.description: Esther Ayomide Adepoju is a researcher and food security advocate who believes Africa can feed Africa. item.description: Governor of Enugu State, Dr. Peter Mbah, on Friday joined other well-meaning Nigerians in celebrating former Minister of Power, Prof. Bart Nnaji, on his 70th birthday, commending the Professor of Robotics and Computer-Integrated Manufacturing for coming home to impact the nation. Abu Itisar), over the recent kidnap of children and teachers from a school in Oriire Local Government Area (LGA) of Oyo State. Th

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The African Democratic Congress (ADC) has said the leadership crisis in the Border Communities Development Agency (BCDA) is an indication that President Bola Tinubu has allegedly “lost control” of his administration. item.title: Anglican church elects four new bishops
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Church of Nigeria (Anglican Communion), has elected four new bishops to oversee the affairs of the dioceses of Makurdi, Uyo, Ekiti Oke and Awori. The post Anglican church elects four new bishops appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Pharmacy Council of Nigeria, PCN, has declared a public health emergency in Cross River after discovering that nearly half of all medicine outlets in the state are operating illegally. Th

item.title: Sheinbaum, Carney to Watch World Cup Final With Trump
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney will travel to the US to attend Sunday’s World Cup final on President Donald Trump’s invitation, creating the opportunity for a reset after a month filled with regional tensions.

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: US President Donald Trump joins criticism of England's tactics in their World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina, by questioning Harry Kane's second-half "defensive" role.

item.description: Mohsen Rezaee has warned that if the US continues bombing Iran, Tehran will escalate its response into full-scale war.

item.description: At least 20 schools have now been completely destroyed in southern Lebanon, with 100 more damaged.

item.title: Trump threatens new Canada tariffs over fires sending 'filthy' air into US cities
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Canadian leader Mark Carney says both the US and Canada have an equal responsibility to fight climate change, which experts say are worsening wildfire conditions.

item.title: SEGUN ODEGBAMI: Who wins – Spain or Argentina?
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The curtains will be drawn on the 2026 FIFA World Cup tomorrow.   The 104 football battles fought amongst 48 countries will come to a most befitting climax. The two teams at the finish line are amongst the 7 that have previously won the World Cup, maintaining a tradition that only a few co

item.title: 2026 World Cup final – Messi vs Yamal: Sorcerer and the apprentice, by Stephanie Shaakaa
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: ​The 2026 FIFA World Cup final on July 19th is not merely a match; it is a funeral for the game as we knew it.  Every civilization has a moment that historians later recognize as the quiet beginning of a new age. It rarely arrives with the fanfare of revolution. More often, it wears the [&#8230

item.title: Moonstone: Modern, cross-platform Lua runtime and package manager written in Zig
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://moonstone.sh/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48954175 Points: 28 # Comments: 8

item.title: Meet Kimi K3, the newest Chinese AI model haunting Silicon Valley
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Moonshot AI’s new open model is catching up to Anthropic and OpenAI, raising questions about the future of the AI race.

item.title: 2026 FIFA World Cup final: History asking for a signature
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: This is not just a game. This is history asking for a signature. And if it happens? A second straight World Cup for Argentina — the very first in the modern era to defend the crown. The post 2026 FIFA World Cup final: History asking for a signature appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: I Started a "Dirt Notebook"
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://pinewind.bearblog.dev/i-started-a-dirt-notebook/ Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48954149 Points: 36 # Comments: 27

item.title: Umahi: How Mary, not Habila, destroyed a powerful senator, by Emmanuel Aziken
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: It was not merely the accident at Chappaquiddick that destroyed Senator Ted Kennedy’s lifelong ambition of becoming President of the United States. It was Mary. Mary Jo Kopechne. She was the young woman who died after Kennedy’s car plunged from a narrow bridge on Chappaquiddick Island in July 1969.

item.title: Israel: The scales are falling off, by Muyiwa Adetiba
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Theirs was a classic Father/Daughter story. She was the apple of his eyes. His Miss Nigeria if not Miss Universe. He was her knight in shining armor. The one person who could do no wrong. She sat on his knees into her teens – much longer than many puritans would deem acceptable. He was putty i
